title: Uniface 10.3.01: Consolidated Updates since 10.2.01
text: Uniface 10.3.01: Consolidated Updates since 10.2.01 Uniface 10.3.01 consolidates new functionality and bug fixes that have been provided in patches since 10.2.02. New Functionality Repository Versioning When running the Uniface IDE from the command line, Uniface first checks the Repository version. If the Repository contains unmigrated data, or data from an incompatible version of the Repository, command line execution will fail. In this case, an error ( -1 ) is returned, and a message sent to the transcript window or log file. For more information, see Repository Versioning . Object Modification Date Using $ude("exist") to check for the existence of compiled objects now returns information about the compiled file in $procReturnContext . This information includes the modification date which can be used to find out when the object was last compiled. This feature is useful if you formerly used the UTIMESTAMP and UCOMPSTAMP fields to identify objects that needed to be compiled after modification. For more information, see $ude ("exist") . Mobile App Enhancements Uniface mobile apps add an extra string containing the text UnifaceMobile to the user-agent HTTP request header. This can be queried and used to provide conditional processing, based on whether the client is running in a mobile environment or a web environment. For more information, see HTTP Headers . Uniface mobile apps for Android now support extra large icon sizes (XXHDPI and XXXHDPI), which can be used for high-definition launcher icons. Testing Enhancements It is now possible to test all types of components from within the Component Editor of the Uniface IDE. To do so, compile the component, then choose Actions >Test to run the component in a separate process. By setting test logicals in the ide.asn file, you can customize the test environment, providing command line switches that override the default behavior. For more information, see Test a Component or Application Shell and IDE Test Logicals . Customizing the IDE The logical IDETITLE enables you to specify your own title for the Uniface IDE. It provides the same functionality as IDFTITLE in Uniface 9. For more information, see Uniface IDE Logicals . Widget Enhancements It is now possible for the Output Box widget to subscribe to multiple channels by setting the MsgChannel property in ProcScript to a comma-delimited list of numeric values representing the desired message channels. For more information, see Output Channel ( MsgChannel) . Support for testing using the Microsoft Active Accessibility (MSAA) API has been extended to the following widgets: Color Box Combo Box Uniface Command Button Dropdown List Label List Box Output Box Grid widget To enable use of the API, set the Accessiblity=TestMode in the usys.ini file. For more information, see Accessibility . If the HTML widget is used to display a URL, it checks certificates of the requested web site. This is a change in behavior enforced by the Chromium Embedded Engine (CEF3) used by the HTML widget. For more information, see uhtml (HTML Widget) . Web Security It is now possible to explicitly declare an operation or trigger as being accessible to web clients and/or SOAP clients. The public web declaration can now be used in operations and triggers of Static Server Pages (USPs) and Service components. It has always been available for Dynamic Server Pages (DSPs). The new public soap declaration can be used in services, DSPs, and USPs. For more information, see soap and web . Migration Enhancements When migrating to Uniface 10, Uniface adds an end statement to close ProcScript modules only when this is required. Previously it did so for all triggers and ProcScript modules, which could result in double end statements. For more information, see Migration of ProcScript and Uniface IDE Logicals . Compiler warning Proc statements occur after 'end' instruction has been replaced. See Warning: 1000 - Script fragment seen outside a module declaration. When importing an XML file or copying entities using entitycopy with a mapping file, problems with specific mappings are reported in $procReturnContext after $ude and entitycopy . When migrating to Uniface 10, the mapping messages may indicate problems with the import file. The log of an import action provides a summary message showing any errors that may have occurred. The message also mentions mapping-related issues that may have occurred during the import. For more information, see Mapping Files , Migrating Repository Objects , and Import and Migration Messages . Bug Fixes Issue 31086: Behavior of AsynchGui=2 (Flush) The behavior of AsynchGui =2 (Flush) has been improved. Only paint messages are dispatched to Windows. User input changes are dispatched when Uniface becomes idle. For more information, see AsynchGui . Issue 31200: Headers sent with UHTTP.SEND When using the UHTTP.SEND operation, headers are now sent with all HTTP request methods, not just POST and PUT . As a result, there is no need to specify this behavior with the SET_FLAGS operation. For more information, see SEND and SET_FLAGS . Issue 31270: HTML widget content is UTF8 Uniface sends content as UTF8. If the content assigned to the HTML widget contains the charset meta tag, it should also be set to UTF8 to ensure that browser displays the data correctly. For more information, see uhtml (HTML Widget) . Issue 31653: IncludeScript migration In Uniface 10, the size of IncludeScript module and library names increased to 60 bytes from 16 bytes in Uniface 9. In some cases, this may cause compilation to fail. For more information, see Migration of IncludeScript . Issue 31654: $processinfo heapsize Windows maintains several memory heaps, so under certain conditions, $processinfo("heapsize") returns an incorrect value. For more information, see $processinfo . Issue 31666: webget requires explicit permission to add occurrences created on the client The behavior of webgget has been made more strict, preventing occurrences created on the client from being added without explicit permission. This new behavior can be enabled by setting the assignment setting $USP_CHECK_NEW_OCC before compiling the USP component. For more information, see webget and $USP_CHECK_NEW_OCC . Issue 31683: TXT connector supports larger record sizes The TXT connector now supports larger record sizes for both the 1.0 and 2.0 versions of the connector. For entities that contain only fixed-length fields, the maximum record length has been increased to 2,147,483,647 bytes. For entities that contain variable-length fields, (C*, W*, and so on), it is increased to 32,767 bytes. However, to take advantage of this increase, you must modify the Database Interface property of the entity to C*.32 and recompile the components that use it. Otherwise the limit remains 8196 bytes. CAUTION: Do not do this if there are existing overflow records. Changing the record length renders these records unreadable. For more information, see Entity Definitions for TXT Files . Issue 31687: New CSS style selectors for DSP Picture widgets Two new CSS style selectors for DSP Picture widgets are introduced: -ufld-no-image- -ufld-broken-image-link- These enable you to control how a Picture field is displayed if it is empty or the specified image cannot be found on the web server. For more information, see Picture . Issue 31689: Trigger migration Default trigger behavior will be applied when the compiler encounters non-executable contents for the trigger. The migration utility has been changed and simplified and the migration logical MIGRATION_KEEP_COMMENT_IN_EMPTY_TRIGGERS is no longer applicable. For more information, see Triggers with Default Behavior and Uniface IDE Logicals . Issue 31701: Migration of model-deefined entries When importing and migrating from Uniface 9 to Uniface 10, the migration facility now generates pre-compiler directives to ensure that model-defined entries that were not compiled in Uniface 9, are also not compiled in Uniface 10. You can prevent the facility from generating this extra, and perhaps unnecessary, code in your application by setting the logical MIGRATION_SCRIPT_V9_INHERITANCE = 0 in the assignment file. See Uniface 10: Changes in ProcScript Inheritance and Inheritance and Compilation of Migrated Entries . Issue 31703: Behavior of OnChange web trigger The OnChange web trigger responds only to user actions, not to data changes made using the JavaScript API. For more information, see webtrigger OnChange . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
